session.user:
# /erk:objective-plan
Create an implementation plan for a specific node in an objective's dependency graph.
## Usage
```bash /erk:objective-plan 3679 /erk:objective-plan 3679 --node 2.1 /erk:objective-plan https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/3679 /erk:objective-plan # prompts for objective reference ```
---
## Agent Instructions
### Step 0: Check for Known Node (Fast Path)
Parse `9109` for `--node <node-id>`. If `--node` is present along with an issue number:
**Invoke the inner skill immediately** via the Skill tool:
``` /erk:system:objective-plan-node <objective-number> --node <node-id> ```
This skips the interactive selection flow (Steps 1-4 below) since the node is already known. The inner skill handles marker creation, marking as planning, context gathering, plan mode, and saving.
**STOP here** — do not proceed to the steps below.
### Step 1: Resolve Objective Reference
Strip `--node` from `9109` if present, then run:
```bash erk exec resolve-objective-ref 9109 ```
**Interpret the JSON result:**
- If `"resolved": true` with `"source": "branch_name"` or `"source": "plan_metadata"`: Inform user "Using objective #N from {source}. Run with explicit argument to override." - If `"resolved": true` with `"source": "argument"`: Use the `objective_number` directly. - If `"resolved": false`: Use AskUserQuestion to ask "What objective issue should I work from?"
### Step 2: Fetch and Set Up Objective Context
Run:
```bash erk exec objective-plan-setup <objective-number> --session-id "0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2" ```
**Interpret the JSON result:**
- If `"success": false` with `"error": "not_found"`: Report error and exit. - If `"success": false` with `"error": "is_plan"`: Redirect to `/erk:plan-implement`. - If `"success": false` with `"error": "validation_error"`: Report error and exit. - If `"success": true`: Continue with the roadmap data.
Display any `"warnings"` to the user.
### Step 3: Load Objective Skill
Load the `objective` skill for format templates and guidance.
### Step 4: Display Roadmap and Prompt User
Display the roadmap table from the `roadmap.phases` data in the JSON output.
Then use AskUserQuestion to ask which node to plan:
``` Which node should I create a plan for? - Node 1A.1: <description> (Recommended) ← first pending node without plan in progress - Node 1A.2: <description> - Node 2B.1: <description> (plan in progress, #456) ← shown but not recommended - (Other - specify node number or description) ```
**Filtering rules (based on node `status` field from phases):**
- **Show as options:** Nodes with status `"pending"` - **Show but deprioritize:** Nodes with status `"in_progress"` - still selectable via "Other" but not recommended - **Hide from options:** Nodes with status `"done"`, `"blocked"`, or `"skipped"`
**Recommendation rule:** Use `roadmap.next_node` from the JSON as the recommended option. If `next_node` is null, no node is recommended.
If all nodes are complete or have plans in progress, report appropriately:
- All complete: "All roadmap nodes are complete! Consider closing the objective." - All have plans: "All pending nodes have plans in progress. You can still select one via 'Other' to create a parallel plan."
### Step 5: Invoke Inner Skill
After the user selects a node, invoke the inner skill via the Skill tool:
``` /erk:system:objective-plan-node <objective-number> --node <selected-node-id> ```
The inner skill handles marker creation, marking as planning, context gathering, plan mode, and saving. **STOP here** — the inner skill takes over.
---
## Output Format
- **Start:** "Loading objective #<number>..." - **After parsing:** Display roadmap nodes with status - **After selection:** "Creating plan for node <step-id>: <description>" - **In plan mode:** Show plan content - **End:** Always proceed with `/erk:plan-save` — do not offer direct implementation
---
## Error Cases
| Scenario | Action | | --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | | Objective not found | Report error and exit | | Reference is erk-plan | Redirect to `/erk:plan-implement` | | No pending nodes | Report all nodes complete, suggest closing | | Invalid argument format | Prompt for valid issue number | | Roadmap not parseable | Ask user to specify which node to plan | | Verification fails (no objective_issue) | `/erk:plan-save` handles automatically; follow remediation steps |
---
## Important Notes
- **Objective context matters:** Read the full objective for design decisions and lessons learned - **One node at a time:** Each plan should focus on a single roadmap node - **Link back:** Always reference the parent objective in the plan - **Steelthread pattern:** If planning a Phase A node, focus on minimal vertical slice
## Post-Plan Behavior
**CRITICAL: Objective plans must ALWAYS be saved as PRs.** When the exit-plan-mode hook presents save-or-implement options, ALWAYS proceed with "Create a plan PR" by running `/erk:plan-save`. Do NOT offer to implement directly — objective node plans require PR tracking for the objective's roadmap status to work correctly.
session.tool_result: {"resolved": true, "objective_number": 9109, "source": "argument"}

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Fetch and set up objective context for #9109

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "objective": {"number": 9109, "title": "Objective: Rename \"plan\" terminology to \"pr\" across all APIs", "state": "OPEN", "labels": ["erk-objective"]}, "roadmap": {"phases": [{"number": 1, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 1", "nodes": [{"id": "1.1", "slug": "rename-gateway-abc", "description": "Rename gateway ABC: PlanService \u2192 PrService, plan_service/ \u2192 pr_service/, method params (plan_id \u2192 pr_number, plan_url \u2192 pr_url)", "status": "done", "pr": "#9110", "depends_on": [], "comment": null}, {"id": "1.2", "slug": "rename-plan-store-types", "description": "Rename plan_store types: Plan.plan_identifier \u2192 pr_identifier, CreatePlanResult.plan_id \u2192 pr_id, PlanNotFound.plan_id \u2192 pr_id, "rename-prompt-executor-types", "description": "Rename prompt executor types: PlanNumberEvent \u2192 PrNumberEvent, CommandResult.plan_number \u2192 pr_number", "status": "done", "pr": "#9110", "depends_on": ["1.2"], "comment": null}, {"id": "1.4", "slug": "rename-output-types", "description": "Rename output types: PlanNextSteps.plan_number \u2192 pr_number, format functions", "status": pipeline, implement, and core modules (~18 files): LandState.plan_id, TargetInfo.plan_number and all consumers", "status": "done", "pr": "#9178", "depends_on": ["3.5"], "comment": null}, {"id": "3.6.2", "slug": "sweep-submit-dispatch-refs", "description": "Sweep plan_id/plan_url/plan_title refs in submit pipeline, dispatch, and PlanContext (~12 files): SubmitState.plan_id, plan_number/plan_id refs in exec scripts and remaining leaf commands (~20 files): self-contained local variable/parameter renames", "status": "done", "pr": "#9186", "depends_on": ["3.6.2"], "comment": null}]}, {"number": 4, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 4", "nodes": [{"id": "4.1", "slug": "update-test-fixtures", "description": "Update test fixtures and assertions for renamed fields and types", "depends_on": ["4.3"], "comment": null}]}, {"number": 5, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 5", "nodes": [{"id": "5.1", "slug": "sweep-plan-store-params", "description": "Semantic rename of plan_store ABC: importers/callers", "status": "done", "pr": "#9198", "depends_on": ["4.4"], "comment": null}, {"id": "5.2", "slug": "sweep-gateway-params", "description": "Rename plan_number/plan_id params across gateway layer: metadata/core.py (change serialized YAML key plan_number to pr_number), command_executor, issues, objective_issues (incl. CreateObjectiveIssueResult field rename), parsing.py, github/abc.py, github/real.py", "status": "done", "pr": "#9238", "depends_on": ["5.1"], "comment": null}, {"id": "5.3", "slug": erk-statusline/statusline.py + test", "status": "done", "pr": "#9274", "depends_on": ["5.2"], "comment": null}]}, {"number": 6, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 6", "nodes": [{"id": "6.1", "slug": "sweep-cli-tui-params", "description": "Rename internal plan_number/plan_id variables/params across CLI commands (29 files) and TUI (4 files) in src/erk/", "status": "in_progress", "pr": "#9277", "depends_on": ["5.3"], "comment": null}, {"id": "6.2", "slug": "rename-remaining-classes", "description": "Rename PlanStatus to PrStatus (status_data.py, orchestrator.py, impl.py) and PlanNextSteps to PrNextSteps (next_steps.py + test)", "status": "in_progress", "pr": "#9277", "depends_on": ["6.1"], "comment": null}]}, {"number": 7, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 7", "nodes": [{"id": "7.1", "slug": "sweep-workflow-files", "description": "Rename plan_id/plan_title/PLAN_ID/PLAN_TITLE in workflow YAML: plan-implement.yml, one-shot.yml, consolidate-learn-plans.yml, pr-address.yml", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["6.2"], "comment": null}, {"id": "7.2", "slug": "sweep-remaining-tests", "description": "Update test fixtures and assertions for all phase 5-6 renames (25 test files, ~89 occurrences)", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["7.1"], "comment": null}, {"id": "7.3", "slug": "cleanup-stale-artifacts", "description": "Delete empty packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_service/ directory (only __pycache__ remains)", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["7.2"], "comment": null}]}, {"number": 8, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 8", "nodes": [{"id": "8.1", "slug": "delete-get-pr-for-plan", "description": "Delete get-pr-for-plan exec command (redundant \u2014 plan number IS the PR number). Migrate callers to get-plan-info, add head_ref_name/base_ref_name to get-plan-info if needed. Delete script, tests, update docs.", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["7.3"], "comment": null}, {"id": "8.2", "slug": "rename-cli-flags-plan-to-pr", "description": "Rename CLI flags where \"plan\" is used as PR identifier: --for-plan \u2192 --for-pr (branch checkout/create), --from-plan \u2192 --from-pr (wt create), --plan \u2192 --pr (duplicate_check, objective_fetch_context), --learn-plan \u2192 --learn-pr (track_learn_result), get-plans-for-objective \u2192 get-prs-for-objective. Update TUI hardcoded --for-plan strings in plan_detail_screen.py.", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["8.1"], "comment": null}]}], "summary": {"total_nodes": 37, "pending": 5, "planning": 0, "done": 30, "in_progress": 2, "blocked": 0, "skipped": 0}, "next_node": {"id": "7.1", "slug": "sweep-workflow-files", "description": "Rename plan_id/plan_title/PLAN_ID/PLAN_TITLE in workflow YAML: plan-implement.yml, one-shot.yml, consolidate-learn-plans.yml, pr-address.yml", "phase": "Phase 7", "status": "pending"}, "all_complete": false}, "validation": {"passed": true, "checks": [{"passed": true, "description": "Issue has erk-objective label"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Roadmap parses successfully"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Status/PR consistency"}, {"passed": true, "description": "No orphaned done statuses (done steps have PR references)"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Phase numbering is sequential"}, {"passed": true, "description": "objective-header has objective_comment_id"}, {"passed": true, "description": "PR references use '#' prefix"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Roadmap table in sync with YAML"}, {"passed": true, "description": "PR backlinks: all PR references have matching objective_issue backlinks"}]}, "marker_created": true, "warnings": []}
Launching skill: objective
session.user:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-12/.claude/skills/objective
# Objective Skill
## Overview
Objectives are coordination documents for goals requiring multiple plans/PRs to complete. Unlike erk-plans (single executable implementations), objectives track progress across related work and capture lessons learned along the way.
**Scope range:**
- Small: Feature requiring 2-3 related PRs - Medium: Refactor spanning several plans - Large: Long-running strategic direction emitting many plans
## Objective vs Erk-Plan
| Aspect | Erk-Plan | Objective | | -------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | | Purpose | Single executable implementation | Coordinate 2+ related plans/PRs | | Scope | One PR or tightly-coupled change | Multiple plans toward coherent goal | | Body | Machine-parseable metadata | Human-readable markdown | | Comments | Session context dumps | Action logs + lessons | | Label | `erk-plan` | `erk-objective` | | Tooling | `erk pr dispatch/implement` | Manual updates via comments |
## Key Design Principles
1. **Human-first** - Plain markdown, no machine-generated metadata 2. **Incremental capture** - Each action gets its own comment 3. **Lessons as first-class** - Every action comment includes lessons learned 4. **Clear roadmap** - Status visible at a glance in the body 5. **Body stays current via reconciliation** - After every PR landing, agents audit prose sections against what was actually implemented and correct stale information 6. **Steelthread-first** - Each phase starts with minimal vertical slice proving the concept works 7. **One PR per phase** - Each phase is sized for a coherent single PR 8. **Always shippable** - System remains functional after each merged PR 9. **Body is source of truth** - Body always contains complete current state; comments are the changelog 10. **Two-step for all changes** - Every addition (context, decisions, phases) gets a comment AND body update 11. **Context over code** - Provide references to patterns, not prescriptive implementations 12. **Session handoff ready** - Body should be self-contained for any session to pick up and implement
## Quick Reference
### Creating an Objective
```bash gh issue create --title "Objective: [Title]" --label "erk-objective" --body "$(cat <<'EOF' # Objective: [Title]
> [1-2 sentence summary]
## Goal
[What success looks like - concrete end state]
## Design Decisions
1. **[Decision name]**: [What was decided]
## Roadmap
### Phase 1: [Name] Steelthread (1 PR)
Minimal vertical slice proving the concept works.
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 1.1 | [Minimal infrastructure] | pending | | | 1.2 | [Wire into one command] | pending | |
**Test:** [End-to-end acceptance test for steelthread]
### Phase 2: Complete [Name] (1 PR)
Fill out remaining functionality.
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 2.1 | [Extend to remaining commands] | pending | | | 2.2 | [Full test coverage] | pending | |
**Test:** [Full acceptance criteria]
EOF )" ```
### Logging an Action
Post an action comment after completing work. See [format.md](references/format.md#action-comment-template) for full template.
```bash gh issue comment <issue-number> --body "$(cat <<'EOF' ## Action: [Brief title] **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD | **PR:** #123 | **Phase/Node:** 1.2 ### What Was Done ### Lessons Learned ### Roadmap Updates EOF )" ```
After posting, update the issue body (roadmap statuses, reconcile stale prose sections).
### Updating Node Details
Update node description, slug, or reason using `update-objective-node`:
```bash # Update description erk exec update-objective-node 8470 --node 1.3 --description "Revised description"
# Set slug erk exec update-objective-node 8470 --node 1.3 --slug "revised-slug"
# Skip with comment erk exec update-objective-node 8470 --node 1.3 --status skipped --comment "Superseded by new approach"
# Combine multiple updates erk exec update-objective-node 8470 --node 2.1 --description "New desc" --slug "new-slug" --status planning ```
### Adding Nodes
Add new nodes to an existing objective's roadmap:
```bash # Add to existing phase (auto-assigns next ID, e.g., 1.4) erk exec add-objective-node 8470 --phase 1 --description "Clean up dead code"
# With explicit slug and dependencies erk exec add-objective-node 8470 --phase 2 \ --description "Integration tests" \ --slug integration-tests \ --depends-on 2.1 --depends-on 2.2
# With comment for adding erk exec add-objective-node 8470 --phase 1 \ --description "Handle edge case" \ --comment "Discovered during re-evaluation" ```
### Updating Node Status
Always use the programmatic command — never manually edit YAML or prose tables:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <issue> --node <id> --status <status> erk exec update-objective-node <issue> --node <id> --pr '#1234' ```
Then validate: `erk objective check <issue-number>`
### Viewing an Objective
View an objective's dependency graph, dependencies, and next node:
```bash # CLI erk objective view <issue-number>
# Slash command (in-session) /local:objective-view <issue-number> ```
### Spawning an Erk-Plan
To implement a specific roadmap node, create an erk-plan that references the objective:
```bash erk pr create --file plan.md --title "Implement [node description]" ```
## Workflow Summary
1. **Create objective** - When starting multi-plan work 2. **Inspect progress** - View dependency graph and next node 3. **Log actions** - After completing each significant piece of work 4. **Update body** - Keep roadmap status current, reconcile stale prose after each PR landing 5. **Spawn erk-plans** - For individual implementation nodes 6. **Close** - When goal achieved or abandoned (proactively ask when all nodes done)
## Resources
### references/
- `format.md` - Complete templates, examples, and update patterns - `workflow.md` - Creating objectives, spawning plans, steelthread structuring - `updating.md` - Quick reference for the two-step update workflow - `closing.md` - Closing triggers and procedures
session.assistant:
**Objective #9109: Rename "plan" terminology to "pr" across all APIs**
Progress: 30/37 nodes done, 2 in progress, 5 pending
### Roadmap
| Phase | Node | Description | Status | |-------|------|-------------|--------| | **Phase 6** | 6.1 | Rename internal plan_number/plan_id variables/params across CLI (29 files) and TUI (4 files) | in_progress (#9277) | | | 6.2 | Rename PlanStatus to PrStatus and PlanNextSteps to PrNextSteps | in_progress (#9277) | | **Phase 7** | **7.1** | **Rename plan_id/plan_title/PLAN_ID/PLAN_TITLE in workflow YAML files** | **pending** | | | 7.2 | Update test fixtures and assertions for phase 5-6 renames (25 test files, ~89 occurrences) | pending | | | 7.3 | Delete empty packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_service/ directory | pending | | **Phase 8** | 8.1 | Delete get-pr-for-plan exec command (redundant) | pending | | | 8.2 | Rename CLI flags where "plan" is used as PR identifier | pending |

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': 'Which node should I create a plan for?', 'header': 'Node', 'options': [{'la...[truncated 488 chars]...n exec command (redundant) and migrate callers to get-plan-info'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result: User has answered your questions: "Which node should I create a plan for?"="Node 7.1 (Recommended)". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.user: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
session.assistant: Creating plan for node 7.1: Rename plan_id/plan_title/PLAN_ID/PLAN_TITLE in workflow YAML files.

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param: Explore workflow YAML files for plan_ references

tool_use.name: Agent

Working directory: /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-12

session.tool_result:
I now have a complete picture. Let me compile the full analysis.
---
Here is a comprehensive breakdown of all `plan_id`, `plan_title`, `PLAN_ID`, `PLAN_TITLE`, and related variables across the four target workflow files — plus the other affected files.
---
## plan-implement.yml
`/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-12/.github/workflows/plan-implement.yml`
### Declared inputs (workflow_dispatch + workflow_call — both blocks)
| Line | Current name | Usage | Rename to | |------|-------------|-------|-----------| | 7, 52 | `plan_id` (input) | "Plan identifier to implement" | `pr_number` | | 19, 65 | `plan_title` (input) | "Plan title for workflow run display" | `pr_title` |
Note: `pr_number` is already a separate input on lines 27/73, meaning after renaming `plan_id` → `pr_number`, the two inputs would clash. The existing `pr_number` input is the implementation PR number (the code PR), while `plan_id` is the plan PR/issue number. This is the conceptual distinction that needs to be reconciled. Based on context from the broader rename effort (the run-name already uses `inputs.pr_number` for the impl PR), the rename for `plan_id` would likely be something like `plan_pr_number` or simply `pr_number` if the existing `pr_number` input is retired or renamed.
### Body references to `PLAN_ID` / `PLAN_TITLE` env vars and `inputs.plan_id` / `inputs.plan_title`
| Line | Token | Context | |------|-------|---------| | 98 | `inputs.plan_id` | `group: implement-plan-${{ inputs.plan_id }}` — concurrency group | | 151 | `PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}` | env in "Checkout implementation branch" step | | 166 | `"$PLAN_ID"` | `erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan "$PLAN_ID"` | | 169 | `"$PLAN_ID"` | `gh pr comment "$PLAN_ID" --body "Could not fetch plan content for plan #$PLAN_ID."` | | 171 | `"$PLAN_ID"` | `gh issue comment "$PLAN_ID" --body "Could not fetch plan content for plan #$PLAN_ID."` |
... [172 lines omitted] ...

I now have a complete picture. Here is a comprehensive report of all the places that reference `plan_id`, `plan_title`, `PLAN_ID`, and `PLAN_TITLE` and what needs to change if those workflow input names are renamed.
---
## Summary of Findings
### 1. Python code that dispatches workflows with `plan_id` / `plan_title` as input names
These are the production-code callsites that build the `inputs` dict passed to `trigger_workflow` / `dispatch_workflow`. Renaming the workflow inputs requires renaming the string keys here.
**`/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-12/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py`**
Two separate callsites, both pass `"plan_id"` and `"plan_title"` as input keys: - Line 287/289 — main dispatch path (uses `ctx.github.trigger_workflow`) - Line 514/516 — remote dispatch path (uses `remote.dispatch_workflow`)
Both build an `inputs` dict with: ```python "plan_id": str(pr_number), "plan_title": validated.title, ```
**`/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-12/src/erk/cli/commands/admin.py`**
Lines 527/530 — test workflow dispatch (used by `erk admin test-plan-implement-gh-workflow`): ```python "plan_id": str(pr_number), "plan_title": "Test workflow run", ```
... [60 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Let me read the key files to understand the `plan_id`/`pr_number` collision before writing the plan.

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

380→ git log --oneline "$PRE_IMPL_HEAD"..HEAD 381→ fi 382→ echo "has_changes=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT 383→ fi 384→ 385→ - name: Submit branch with proper commit message 386→ id: submit 387→ continue-on-error: true 388→ if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' 389→ env: 390→ CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }} 391→ ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }} 392→ GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }} 393→ PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }} 394→ BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }} 395→ SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }} 396→ run: | 397→ git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY" 398→ git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com" 399→ claude --print \ 400→ --model ${{ inputs.model_name }} \ 401→ --output-format stream-json \ 402→ --dangerously-skip-permissions \ 403→ --verbose \ 404→ "/erk:git-pr-push Implement plan #$PLAN_ID" 405→ echo "impl_sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT 406→ 407→ - name: Handle merge conflicts if push failed 408→ id: handle_conflicts 409→ if: steps.submit.outcome == 'failure'
... [65 lines omitted] ...

PLAN SAVE PROMPT

Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Node 7.1 — Rename plan_id/plan_title/PLAN_ID/PLAN_TITLE in Workflow YAML Current context: (wt:erk-slot-12) (br:plnd/O9109-rename-cli-tui-plan-03-20-1505) (pr:#9277)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:plnd/O910"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch. Read the plan from: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/dreamy-wishing-lamport.md Implement changes, run CI, and optionally 'erk pr submit' when done.

session.assistant:
Here is the plan:
---
# Plan: Node 7.1 — Rename plan_id/plan_title/PLAN_ID/PLAN_TITLE in Workflow YAML
## Context
This is node 7.1 of Objective #9109 (Rename "plan" terminology to "pr" across all APIs). Phases 1–6 renamed Python types, ABC methods, Click params, exec scripts, and core modules. This node cleans up the last layer: GitHub Actions workflow YAML files.
The four files named in the node description (`plan-implement.yml`, `one-shot.yml`, `consolidate-learn-plans.yml`, `pr-address.yml`) are the primary targets. Exploration also found `learn.yml`, `pr-rebase.yml`, and `pr-rewrite.yml` using the same local env var pattern — these are included to complete the sweep.
Python dispatch code and their tests are also in scope because they pass these names as workflow input keys.
## Collision Resolution (Important)
`plan-implement.yml` currently has **two** separate inputs: - `plan_id` — the erk-plan PR being implemented (rename → `pr_number`) - `pr_number` — the implementation code branch's PR (rename → `impl_pr_number`)
Renaming `plan_id` → `pr_number` would collide with the existing `pr_number` input. Resolution: rename the existing `pr_number` input (and all its uses) to `impl_pr_number` simultaneously.
## Changes
### 1. `.github/workflows/plan-implement.yml`
**Input declarations** (both `workflow_dispatch` and `workflow_call` blocks): - `plan_id` → `pr_number` (lines 7 and 52) - `plan_title` → `pr_title` (lines 19 and 65) - existing `pr_number` → `impl_pr_number` (lines 27 and 73)
**Concurrency group** (line 98): `implement-plan-${{ inputs.plan_id }}` → `implement-plan-${{ inputs.pr_number }}`
**All `PLAN_ID` env var declarations** → `PR_NUMBER` (lines 151, 187, 267, 287, 303, 336, 393)
**All `PLAN_TITLE` env var declarations** → `PR_TITLE` (line 337)
**All `$PLAN_ID` shell usages** → `$PR_NUMBER` (lines 166, 169, 171, 180, 193, 253, 296, 309, 404)
**`inputs.plan_id` inline reference** (line 461) → `inputs.pr_number`
**All `inputs.pr_number` references** (impl PR) → `inputs.impl_pr_number`
### 2. `.github/workflows/one-shot.yml`
- Job output keys `plan_id`/`plan_title` → `pr_number`/`pr_title` (lines 73–74) - Shell vars `PLAN_ID`/`PLAN_TITLE` → `PR_NUMBER`/`PR_TITLE` (lines 197–201); `PLAN_NUMBER` → `PR_NUMBER` (line 240) - Step outputs `plan_id`/`plan_title` → `pr_number`/`pr_title` - All `steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id` → `pr_number` (lines 207, 211, 217, 223, 233, 252) - `needs.plan.outputs.plan_id` → `pr_number` (line 294) - `workflow_call` with block: `plan_id:` → `pr_number:`, `plan_title:` → `pr_title:`, `pr_number:` → `impl_pr_number:` - jq expression key `plan_id` → `pr_number` (line 265)
### 3. `.github/workflows/consolidate-learn-plans.yml`
Same pattern as `one-shot.yml`: - Job output keys `plan_id`/`plan_title` → `pr_number`/`pr_title` (lines 44–45) - Shell vars `PLAN_ID`/`PLAN_TITLE` → `PR_NUMBER`/`PR_TITLE` (lines 137–145) - `needs.consolidate.outputs.plan_id` → `pr_number` (line 174) - `workflow_call` with block keys (lines 177–178): `plan_id:` → `pr_number:`, `plan_title:` → `pr_title:`
### 4. `.github/workflows/pr-address.yml`
- `PLAN_NUMBER` env var → `PR_NUMBER` (line 48) - Step output `plan_id` → `pr_number` (line 72) - All `steps.plan_info.outputs.plan_id` → `pr_number` (lines 129, 145) - `PLAN_ID` env vars → `PR_NUMBER` (lines 133, 149) - `$PLAN_ID` / `$PLAN_NUMBER` shell usages → `$PR_NUMBER` (lines 54, 142, 157)
### 5–7. `learn.yml`, `pr-rebase.yml`, `pr-rewrite.yml`
- `PLAN_ID` / `PLAN_NUMBER` local env vars → `PR_NUMBER` (~2–4 lines each)
### 8. `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py`
Two callsites (lines ~287 and ~514): - `"plan_id"` → `"pr_number"`, `"plan_title"` → `"pr_title"`, `"pr_number"` → `"impl_pr_number"`
### 9. `src/erk/cli/commands/admin.py`
One callsite (lines ~527–534): - `"plan_id"` → `"pr_number"`, `"plan_title"` → `"pr_title"`, `"pr_number"` → `"impl_pr_number"`
### 10–12. Tests
- `tests/commands/pr/test_dispatch.py` — `inputs["plan_id"]` → `inputs["pr_number"]` (3 occurrences); check/update `inputs["pr_number"]` (impl PR) → `inputs["impl_pr_number"]` - `tests/commands/pr/test_remote_paths.py` — `dispatched.inputs["plan_id"]` → `dispatched.inputs["pr_number"]` - `tests/unit/cli/commands/test_admin_test_workflow.py` — `inputs["plan_id"]` → `inputs["pr_number"]`; `inputs["pr_number"]` (draft_pr) → `inputs["impl_pr_number"]`
## Verification
```bash # 1. No remaining plan_id/plan_title/PLAN_ID/PLAN_TITLE in workflow files grep -r "plan_id\|plan_title\|PLAN_ID\|PLAN_TITLE\|PLAN_NUMBER" .github/workflows/ # Expected: zero matches
# 2. Run fast CI (unit tests + lint) make fast-ci ```
---

User has answered your questions: "📋 Plan: Node 7.1 — Rename plan_id/plan_title/PLAN_ID/PLAN_TITLE in Workflow YAML Current context: (wt:erk-slot-12) (br:plnd/O9109-rename-cli-tui-plan-03-20-1505) (pr:#9277)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
Launching skill: erk:plan-save
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `pr_number`, `pr_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `pr_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <pr_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<pr_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<pr_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → PR #<pr_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as PR #<pr_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<pr_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `pr_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --pr-number <pr_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<pr_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
**Branch slug:** `rename-workflow-yaml-plan-vars`
**Plan summary:** Renames plan_id, plan_title, PLAN_ID, PLAN_TITLE, and PLAN_NUMBER variables across 7 GitHub Actions workflow files and their Python dispatch callers. Resolves the plan_id/pr_number naming collision in plan-implement.yml by renaming the existing pr_number input to impl_pr_number.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2" --bra...[truncated 223 chars]...llision in plan-implement.yml by renaming the existing pr_number input to impl_pr_number."

session.tool_result: Linked to objective #9109 from session context {"success": true, "pr_number": 9282, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9282", "title": "[erk-pr] Plan: Node 7.1 \u2014 Rename plan_id/plan_title/PLAN_ID/PLAN_TITLE in Workflow YAML", "branch_name": "plnd/O9109-rename-workflow-yaml-03-20-1551", "plan_backend": "planned_pr", "objective_issue": 9109, "archived_to": "/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-12/.erk/scratch/sessions/0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2/plan_snapshots/000001-ad240bbf"}

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Verify objective link on saved plan

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "value": 9109, "pr_number": 9282, "field": "objective_issue"}

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "0e824fbf-4d30-4759-b738-7c5d3ebeb5d2" roadmap-step) && echo "$step_id"

session.tool_result: Exit code 1
session.assistant: The roadmap-step marker wasn't set (interactive selection path). I know the node is 7.1, so I'll update the objective directly.

tool_use.name: ExitPlanMode
tool_use.param:

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: PR #9282 saved successfully.
Display ALL of the following next-steps commands to the user as plain text. Show every line exactly as written — do NOT summarize, truncate, or omit any lines. (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — just display the text):
Implement PR #9282: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 9282 && erk implement (dangerously): erk br co --for-plan 9282 && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9282 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9282 --script)" && erk implement -d
Checkout PR #9282: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 9282 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9282
Dispatch PR #9282: CLI command: erk pr dispatch 9282 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch
Session complete. Do NOT call ExitPlanMode again.
session.assistant:
Plan saved. Here are your next steps:
**Implement PR #9282:** ``` In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 9282 && erk implement (dangerously): erk br co --for-plan 9282 && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9282 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9282 --script)" && erk implement -d ```
**Checkout PR #9282:** ``` In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 9282 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9282 ```
**Dispatch PR #9282:** ``` CLI command: erk pr dispatch 9282 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch ```
